scope: Pass B full-surface expansion, verified against the real published OpenAPI 3 spec (https://apidoc.samanage.com/ renders it via ReDoc; the raw machine-readable document is served at https://apidoc.samanage.com/redoc/schema/resolved_schema.json, fetched 2026-07-03: 135 documented operations across 66 paths). The metadata.json docs_url this bundle previously carried (documentation.solarwinds.com/.../swsd-api.htm) 404s live as of this review and has been replaced with the working apidoc.samanage.com reference. 24 read streams (6 legacy-parity: incidents/users/departments/categories/problems/changes, plus 18 newly added: change_catalogs/releases/solutions/catalog_items/configuration_items/sites/roles/groups/hardwares/mobiles/other_assets/softwares/printers/contracts/purchase_orders/vendors/audits/risks) and 20 delete write actions (one per resource with a documented DELETE-by-id endpoint, excluding memberships -- see below) are covered_by a stream or write. create/update (POST/PUT) mutations on every primary resource are blocked by a single recurring ENGINE_GAP: every one of this API's create/update request bodies wraps the payload under a single resource-named root key (e.g. POST /incidents expects {"incident": {...}}, PUT /solutions/{id} expects {"solution": {...}}), and this dialect's write.go has no mechanism to nest the constructed JSON body under a declared root key (body_type 'json' sends every non-path_fields record field flush at the top level; 'form' and 'none' have no analogous namespacing either) -- this is the SAME engine gap already filed against internal/connectors/defs/statuspage/api_surface.json's mutation endpoints in this program (its own docs.md names the same two viable fixes: a Tier-1 writes.json 'body_envelope' field, or an orchestrator-approved WriteHook; this pass's constraints forbid creating a new hook package, so every affected endpoint below is excluded with category requires_elevated_scope carrying an ENGINE_GAP note, following that exact precedent). DELETE endpoints have no request body at all and are unaffected -- every resource's delete-by-id action is implemented.

excluded.reason: single-incident-by-id detail; identical record shape already covered by the incidents list stream's full result set

ENGINE_GAP: request body must be wrapped under a root "incident" key ({"incident": {...}}); this dialect's write.go has no body-envelope/namespacing mechanism (body_type json/form/none all send the record flush, never nested under a declared key) -- same gap as defs/statuspage/docs.md's Write actions & risks section; forbidden to add a new hook package this pass

ENGINE_GAP: takes group_id/user_ids as QUERY parameters with no request body at all -- expressible in principle, but this dialect's writes.json has no declared-query-parameter mechanism (only path/path_fields/body_type/body_fields), so the write path has no field to carry group_id/user_ids into the query string; also account/access-control administration (relates a user to a group), out of remit for a data-sync connector even if it were wired

no GET/list endpoint exists for memberships at all, so there is no stream to source a valid membership id from; a lone delete-by-id with no discovery path is not implementable as a syncable write action
per-parent-record audit-trail filter; a narrower view of the same audit-event data the audits stream already returns organization-wide
requires a multipart/form-data file upload (curl -F 'file[attachment]=@/PATH/TO/FILE'), not a JSON/form record body this dialect's write actions can express
